---
title: "Masking analyses"
author: "Connor Wessel"
execute: 
  echo: false
  message: false
  warning: false
  include: false
---


# Data preprocessing
```{r}
# Load packages
library(tidyverse)
library(brms)
library(ggtext)
library(rstudioapi)

# Relative working directory
active_wd <- function(x, output = FALSE) {
    rdir <- dirname(rstudioapi::getActiveDocumentContext()$path)
    rdir <- sub("[^/]*$", "", rdir)
    rdir <- paste0(rdir, x)
    setwd(rdir)
    if (output == TRUE) {
        return(getwd())
    }
}
active_wd("data")

# Load data
library(tidyverse)
library(brms)
library(ggtext)
data <- read_csv("full_data.csv")
# data <- read_csv("pilot_data.csv")
reversals <- 4 # Specify how many reversals we are averaging across
```

```{r}
# Select final 4 reversals in each staircase for each Participant
last_revs <- data.frame()
for (i in unique(data$Participant)) {
  for (j in unique(data$start_id)) {
    for (k in unique(data$type)) {
      for (l in unique(data$envelope_1)) {
        for (m in unique(data$repetition)) {
          for (n in unique(data$direction)) {
            for (o in unique(data$offset)) {
              rev = subset(data, Participant == i & type == k & envelope_1 == l & repetition == m & direction == n & offset == o & l_reversal == 1)
              if (!j %in% rev$start_id) next
              rev = subset(rev, start_id == j)
              num = length(rev$l_reversal) - reversals
              rev = rev[-c(1:num),]
              last_revs <- rbind(last_revs, rev)
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

```{r}
# Categorise each reversal
k = 1
for (i in 1:nrow(last_revs)) {
  if (k > reversals) k = 1
  last_revs$trial_num[i] <- k
  k = k + 1
}
```

```{r}
# Group and condense response/reversal data
revs <- last_revs %>% 
  group_by(Participant, start_id, direction, envelope_1, type, repetition, offset, trial_num) %>% 
  summarise("Level" = mean(second - first)) %>% 
  mutate("trial_num" = as.integer(trial_num), "start_id" = factor(start_id)) %>% 
  arrange(Participant, start_id, direction, envelope_1, type, repetition, trial_num)
```

```{r}
# Calculate PSE for each standard and envelope for each participant (and Z scores)
pse <- revs %>% 
  group_by(Participant, start_id, envelope_1, type, offset, repetition) %>% 
  summarise("PSE" = mean(Level))
pse$PSEZ <- ((pse$PSE - mean(pse$PSE)) / sd(pse$PSE))

pse$PSEtotal <- 0
pse$standard <- 0
for (i in 1:length(unique(pse$envelope_1))) {
  for (j in 1:length(unique(pse$start_id))) {
    e <- unique(pse$envelope_1)[i]
    if (!j %in% unique(pse$start_id[pse$envelope_1 == e])) next
    pse$PSEtotal[pse$envelope_1 == unique(pse$envelope_1)[i] & pse$start_id == j] <- data$first[data$envelope_1 == unique(pse$envelope_1)[i] & data$start_id == j] + pse$PSE[pse$envelope_1 == unique(pse$envelope_1)[i] & pse$start_id == j]
    pse$standard[pse$envelope_1 == unique(pse$envelope_1)[i] & pse$start_id == j] <- data$first[data$envelope_1 == unique(pse$envelope_1)[i] & data$start_id == j]
  }
}

# Convert relevant columns into factors
pse$Participant <- factor(pse$Participant)
pse$envelope_1 <- factor(pse$envelope_1)
pse$offset <- factor(pse$offset)
```

# Inferential analyses

```{r}
# Inferential analyses

# Set model priors
#   Note - use pilot data in data preprocessing to look at model parameters that informed these priors.
priors <- c(
  prior(normal(-28.7, 0.593), class = "Intercept"),
  prior(normal(-32, 0.69), class = "b", coef = "envelope_1perc"),
  prior(normal(0.472, 0.692), class = "b", coef = "envelope_1revperc"),
  prior(normal(-20.4, 0.693), class = "b", coef = "offset0.405"),
  prior(normal(14.8, 0.974), class = "b", coef = "envelope_1perc:offset0.405"),
  prior(normal(-0.276, 0.978), class = "b", coef = "envelope_1revperc:offset0.405"),
  prior(normal(3.98, 0.157), class = "sigma"),
  prior(normal(2.69, 0.334), class = "sd", coef = "Intercept", group = "Participant")
)

# Uniform priors for pilot data
# priors <- c(
#   prior(uniform(-40, 40), lb = -40, ub = 40, class = "b")
# )

# Run model
model <- brm(
  PSEtotal ~ envelope_1 * offset + (1|Participant),
  data = pse,
  prior = priors,
  family = gaussian(),
  chains = 10,
  cores = 10,
  iter = 10000,
  warmup = 2000,
  sample_prior = "yes",
  save_pars = save_pars(all = TRUE),
  seed = 1
)

# Extract posterior draws for each hypothesis
test <- hypothesis(
  model,
  c(
    "abs(((2*envelope_1perc + envelope_1perc:offset0.405) / 2)) > 0",
    "abs(envelope_1perc:offset0.405) > 0",
    "offset0.405 > envelope_1perc",
    "((2*envelope_1revperc + envelope_1revperc:offset0.405) / 2) = 0"
  )
  )
```

# Figures

## Figure 3

```{r}
# Figure 3 - Example staircase
data %>% 
  filter(Participant == 1 & envelope_1 == "perc" & offset == 0.405) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(trial_num, second)) +
  geom_path(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_point(aes(color = factor(l_reversal))) +
  theme_classic() +
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = first), 
             linetype = 1, 
             alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_hline(data = pse[pse$Participant == i & pse$envelope_1 == "perc" & pse$offset == 0.405,], 
             mapping = aes(yintercept = PSEtotal), 
             color = "purple", 
             alpha = 0.5,
             linetype = 2) +
  geom_text(data = pse[pse$Participant == i & pse$envelope_1 == "perc" & pse$offset == 0.405,], 
            aes(label = paste("Delta*dB==", PSE)), 
            x = 19, 
            y = -75,
            hjust = "left",
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            parse = TRUE) +
  xlab("Trials") +
  ylab("Target intensity (dB)") +
  ylim(c(-80,0)) +
  scale_color_manual("Reversal",
                     labels = c("No", "Yes"),
                     values = c("blue", "red")) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
# ggsave("staircase_p1.svg", width = 6, height = 4)
```

## Figure 4

```{r}
# Figure 4 - Means and standard errors
ebd <- pse |> 
  group_by(envelope_1, offset) |> 
  summarise(m = mean(PSEtotal), se = sd(PSEtotal) / sqrt(length(PSEtotal))) |> 
  mutate(l = m - se, u = m + se)

pd <- position_dodge(0.2)
ebd |> 
  ggplot(aes(
    x = factor(envelope_1, levels = c("flat", "perc", "revperc"), labels = c("Flat", "Percussive", "Reverse percussive")),
    y = m,
    ymin = l,
    ymax = u,
    color = factor(offset)
  )) +
  geom_errorbar(width = 0.6, position = pd, linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_point(pch = 21, fill = "white", position = pd, size = 0.7) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x = "Masker envelope", y = "Threshold (dB)") +
  scale_color_manual("Masking condition", values = c("#ff4e00", "#00b1ff"), labels = c("Simultaneous", "Forward")) +
  theme(legend.position = "right", text = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_markdown()) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    labels = \(x) glue::glue("<img src='{x}.png' height=30 />")
  )
# ggsave("errorbar.svg", width = 6, height = 4.5)
```

## Figure 5

```{r}
# Figure 5 - Beta coefficients
coefs <- data.frame(test$hypothesis)
coefs$Hypothesis <- as.factor(1:4)
levels(coefs$Hypothesis) <- paste0("H[",1:4,"]")
levels(coefs$Hypothesis)[4] <- "Control"
r2 <- bayes_R2(model)[1]

coefs[1:4,] |> 
    ggplot(aes(x = Estimate, y = Hypothesis)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = `CI.Lower`, xmax = `CI.Upper`), width = 0.6) +
    geom_point(pch = 21, fill = "white") +
    theme_classic() +
    scale_y_discrete(limits = levels(coefs$Hypothesis), labels = parse(text = levels(coefs$Hypothesis))) +
    labs(x = expression(hat(beta))) +
    annotate("text", x = 24, y = 4, label = paste0("R^2 == ", round(r2, 2)), parse = TRUE, size = 4.2) +
    xlim(c(0, 27)) +
    coord_flip()
# ggsave("coefs5.svg", width = 6, height = 4)
```
